title: Retail in 2026: Growth, competition, and the case for consumption ...
snippet:
India’s retail sector enters 2026 on firmer footing than it did a year ago. After a cautious start marked by subdued consumption, momentum strengthened steadily through 2025, reflecting a market that is stabilising, recalibrating, and preparing for a more competitive phase of growth.  
  
Retail sales growth stood at around 4-5% year-on-year in the early part of the year, rising to approximately 8% by the second quarter as demand broadened across categories. During the festive period, growth accelerated further to around 11%, signalling renewed consumer confidence, particularly in discretionary segments. This progression does not signal a consumption boom, but measured growth shaped by value awareness, selective spending, and intensifying competition in 2026. [...] By Kumar Rajagopalan, ET CONTRIBUTORS

title: Indian Retail Sector: Growth, Trends, and Future Projections (2026)
snippet:
Encouragingly, the FMCG sector reported a volume-led rebound in the December quarter, aided by GST-related normalisation, festive demand and easing input costs. Rural markets once again outperformed urban centres, while e-commerce emerged as a strong incremental growth driver. This recovery is also setting the stage for higher advertising spends in FY26, with FMCG expected to remain the single largest contributor to India’s AdEx cycle. [...] CPM Logo

# Indian Retail at a Glance 13012026

Jan 13, 2026 6:36:19 AM | CPM International

Featured Image

India’s retail and FMCG landscape closed the year on a note of cautious recovery, with macro signals turning supportive even as structural tensions persist. Retail inflation rose for the second consecutive month to 1.33% in December, driven by higher food and personal care prices, but remained comfortably below the RBI’s 4% target for an eleventh straight month. This benign inflation backdrop is helping stabilise consumption, particularly in essential categories. [...] That said, not all consumption segments are back in form. Goldman Sachs remains cautious on discretionary categories such as fashion, footwear

title: Retail Industry in India: Market Size, Growth & Future Outlook (2026)
snippet:
Technology adoption: AI, IoT, and automation will drive efficient supply chains and hyper-personalized customer engagement. Platforms like TROCCO provide the integrated data foundation needed for accurate, real-time decision-making.  
  ‍
 Organized retail expansion: Malls, supermarkets, and e-commerce will continue to increase their market share, supported by investments from global and local giants.  
  ‍
 Rural penetration: With over 65% of India’s population living in rural areas, retailers are focusing on kirana digitization and ONDC to tap into this demand.  
  ‍
 Sustainability focus: Eco-friendly packaging, green logistics, and ethical sourcing will influence purchase decisions as consumers become more environmentally conscious.  
  ‍ [...] Rising incomes: The expanding middle class is boosting household spending, shifting demand from essentials toward discretionary categories like fashion, electronics, and personal care. This creates opportunities for both modern trade and premium retail formats.  
  ‍
 Urbanization: With cities growing rapidly, consumers are exposed to new lifestyles and shopping experiences. This urban demand fuels the rise of malls, supermarkets, and or

title: Indian Retail Sector Poised for Major Growth by 2026 Amidst E ...
snippet:
The outlook for 2026 for the retail sector is "highly optimistic" with expectations of continued double-digit growth and further transformation, said Anand Ramanathan, Partner & Consumer Industry Leader, Deloitte India.  
  
"GDP growth is projected at 6.4 to 6.7 per cent, inflation is under control, and consumer sentiment remains strong. E-commerce penetration will deepen, with more consumers from smaller towns and rural areas shopping online.  
  
"Quick commerce and social commerce will continue to disrupt traditional models, and omnichannel maturity will enable unified experiences and flexible fulfilment options," he said. [...] "As we enter 2026, India's retail growth remains structurally strong, but the next phase will be margin-led rather than volume-led.  
  
Retailers that combine cost discipline and leverage technology such as AI to deliver greater personalisation and seamless omni-channel execution will emerge as long-term category leaders," he said.  
  
Expressing identically, Retailers Association of India (RAI) CEO Kumar Rajagopalan said the retail sector will enter 2026 with steady momentum built through 2025, marked by measured but broad-based growth rather than ex

title: Market Evaluation: India Retail Market is Driven by a 12.80%
snippet:
Market Overview  
   
 Key statistics extracted from the report page include:  
   
 • Forecast Period: 2026-2034  
 • Base Year: 2025  
 • Historical Years: 2020-2025  
 • Market Size (2025): USD 1,124.2 Billion  
 • Forecast Value (2034): USD 3,505.4 Billion  
 • CAGR: 12.80% during 2026-2034  
   
 The India retail market reached USD 1,124.2 Billion in 2025 and is projected to reach USD 3,505.4 Billion by 2034, reflecting a robust expansion supported by growing consumer spending and the rise of organized retail formats across urban and semi-urban areas.

title: Top 10 Trends Redefining Marketing in 2026
snippet:
Infrastructure upgrades have made shopping far more enjoyable with walkable high streets, better lighting, and even parking spaces, a true urban miracle.

The government’s done its part too, rolling out the red carpet for global brands. India permits 100% FDI in single-brand retail and 51% in multi-brand, which is extremely conducive for brands looking to set up shop here. And the result is clear. India has climbed up the Global Retail Development Index, and luxury brands are now treating the country as a must-enter market. [...] More than half of retail consumers now research products online before buying them in stores. Similarly, many shoppers explore products in stores before eventually purchasing them online.

This shift is encouraging retailers to integrate their digital and physical operations more closely and build unified commerce strategies.

Retailers adopting such strategies are already seeing strong results. According to the report, Indian retailers using Meta’s Omnichannel Optimization tools have seen more than four times improvement in omnichannel return on ad spend (ROAS). Integrated data strategies have also driven revenue growth of up to 15 percent. [...] The repo
